task_completion.description: Open Oracle Analytics Desktop and load the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Oracle Analytics Desktop application window is open, and the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset is visible in the data panel or active project.

task_completion.description: Select the required columns: Customer Name, Customer Segment, and Profit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table or visualization in the main canvas clearly displays the columns 'Customer Name', 'Customer Segment', and 'Profit'.

task_completion.description: Apply a filter to include only the 'Corporate' customer segment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filter bar or panel shows a filter on 'Customer Segment' with the value set to 'Corporate'.

task_completion.description: Apply a filter to include only profits of $5,000 or more.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filter bar or panel shows a filter on 'Profit' with a condition such as '>= 5000' or a range starting from 5000.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export or save process for the filtered data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export menu, save dialog, or 'Export to CSV/File' option is open and actively being used on the filtered visualization.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting data as a CSV file named vip_corporate_customers.csv to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or File Explorer window shows the file being saved with the name 'vip_corporate_customers.csv' in the 'Documents' folder (C:\Users\Docker\Documents\).

integrity.description: The agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to perform the data extraction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface (dragging columns, clicking filters) rather than writing a Python/Pandas script in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent exported the data from the application rather than manually typing fake data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final CSV is generated via the application's export dialog, not by opening Notepad and typing out comma-separated values.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands (like PowerShell or bash) to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown executing scripts to directly query the underlying data files and output the CSV.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output file: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\vip_corporate_customers.csv. Required columns: 'Customer Name', 'Customer Segment', 'Profit'. Filters required: Customer Segment must be 'Corporate', Profit must be >= 5000. The resulting dataset should contain at least 1 row (specifically, there are 20 corporate customers with aggregated profit >= 5000, or 14 individual order lines).
